tool_input.summary: Relevant PDS datasets for MER Spirit (MER-2) observations of the Home Plate silica outcrops (composition/spectra, close-up textures, and color/context imaging). Includes both PDS4 bundles and corresponding PDS3 volumes where available.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Mini-TES emissivity products used to identify the hydrated/opaline silica spectral signature at the Home Plate silica deposits.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MTES-4-EMR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Mini-TES derived emissivity archive corresponding to the Mini-TES spectral identification of silica at/near Home Plate.

candidates.title: MER APXS Derived Oxide Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: APXS-derived oxide abundances document the extreme SiO2 enrichment and depletion of other major elements consistent with a silica precipitate (sinter) rather than unaltered basalt.

candidates.title: MER1/
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 APXS derived oxide volume corresponding to the high-silica bulk chemistry of the Home Plate silica outcrops.

candidates.title: MER2 Microscopic Imager Science Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: MI close-up images provide the key textures (nodular/digitate, porous, crusty surfaces) compared to terrestrial hot-spring sinter/geyserite.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MI-3-RDR-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated MI archive corresponding to the texture observations used in the sinter interpretation.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Science Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Pancam calibrated multispectral imaging constrains the deposit’s albedo/color and geologic context (distribution along/near Home Plate margins), supporting an in-place precipitate interpretation.

candidates.title: MER2-M-PANCAM-3-RADCAL-SCI-V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 radiometrically calibrated Pancam data for the same context/color observations at Home Plate.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What observations of the Home Plate silica outcrops indicated it was a sinter deposited by hot spring or geyser activity?
